title: Catalyzed vs. Uncatalyzed Energy Profile
creator: Dan McCreary
subject: AP Chemistry
description: Interactive energy diagram showing how catalysts lower activation energy without changing reaction enthalpy, with adjustable profiles and animation.

learningObjectives: Explain how catalysts lower the activation energy barrier while leaving ΔH unchanged.
Compare uncatalyzed and catalyzed pathways by reading Ea and ΔH values from energy diagrams.
prerequisites: Definition of activation energy and enthalpy

keyQuestions: How does lowering Ea change the reaction rate?
Why does a catalyst not affect ΔH?
assessmentOpportunities: Students capture the diagram at different catalyst strengths and report Ea values.
Learners describe how the intermediate option models multi-step mechanisms.
